symbols.name: \(O_i\)
symbols.def: Observed right-censored single-initiation illness-death trajectory of unit \(i\).

symbols.name: \(P^{(n)}\)
symbols.def: Row-\(n\) observed-data law in the critical triangular experiment.

symbols.name: \(P_G^{(n)}\)
symbols.def: Row-\(n\) observed-data law in the diverging-effective-count triangular family.

symbols.name: \(O_{ni}\)
symbols.def: Observed trajectory of unit \(i\) in row \(n\) of a triangular family.

symbols.name: \(P_{\mathrm{full}}\)
symbols.def: Full-data causal-law extension with observed margin \(P\).

symbols.name: \(T^u\)
symbols.def: Terminal-event time under suppression before deterministic eligible initiation at \(u\).

symbols.name: \(Y_0(t)\)
symbols.def: Untreated, alive, uncensored eligibility indicator immediately before \(t\).

symbols.name: \(f_{U,P}\)
symbols.def: Marginal natural initiation-time density in the baseline-eligible hazard model, \(f_{U,P}(u)=E_P[S^o_{0,P}(u\mid X)\alpha_{01,P}(u\mid X)]\).

symbols.name: \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{full}}_{t-}\)
symbols.def: Full-data history immediately before \(t\), containing \(\mathcal H_{t-}\).

symbols.name: \(\overline Q_b\)
symbols.def: Probability of no planned initiation by time \(t\).

symbols.name: \(a_b\)
symbols.def: At-risk initiation hazard of the planned rule.

symbols.name: \(A_1\)
symbols.def: Active post-initiation support \(\{(t,u):q_b(u)>0,\overline Q_b(u)>0,u\le t\le t_0\}\).

symbols.name: \(g_P\)
symbols.def: Unique Hölder version of the identified risk equivalence class.

symbols.name: \(R_b(P)\)
symbols.def: Risk under the exogenous at-risk initiation rule.

symbols.name: \(S^b_{0,P}\)
symbols.def: Interventional untreated survival before its absorbing post-window convention.

symbols.name: \(V_{0,P,b}\)
symbols.def: Interventional terminal-risk continuation value from untreated state zero.

symbols.name: \(V_{1,P}\)
symbols.def: Post-initiation terminal-risk continuation value at \(t\) after initiation at \(u\).

symbols.name: \(\phi^q_{P,b}\)
symbols.def: Candidate efficient influence function obtained by direct pathwise differentiation of \(R_b(P)\).

symbols.name: \(\widetilde R_{b_n}\)
symbols.def: Oracle one-step estimator using the true score.

symbols.name: \(\widehat R_{b_n}\)
symbols.def: Cross-fitted one-step estimator using fitted nuisance functions.

symbols.name: \(\widehat R^{(-i)}_{b_n}\)
symbols.def: Training-fold plug-in estimate of the regularized risk.

symbols.name: \(\widehat v_{b_n}\)
symbols.def: Cross-fitted empirical variance estimate of the full score.

symbols.name: \(\widetilde R^{\,G}_{b_n}\)
symbols.def: Oracle one-step estimator in the diverging-effective-count triangular family.

symbols.name: \(s^o_{G,n}\)
symbols.def: Deterministic oracle standard error \(\sqrt{v_{P_G^{(n)},b_n}/n}\) in the diverging-effective-count family.

symbols.name: \(Z^o_{G,n}\)
symbols.def: Deterministically normalized oracle statistic in the diverging-effective-count family.

symbols.name: \(\widehat\Xi_n\)
symbols.def: Localized marked point process formed from held-out fitted scores.

symbols.name: \(p_1\)
symbols.def: Post-initiation terminal-event probability by the horizon in the constant-hazard submodel.

symbols.name: \(c(v)\)
symbols.def: Rescaled post-initiation score weight in the constant-hazard submodel.

symbols.name: \(\xi\)
symbols.def: Generic argument of the marked-point-process maps.

symbols.name: \(\Gamma^{\mathrm{SN}}_\lambda\)
symbols.def: Self-normalized marked-Poisson map with deterministic fallback.

symbols.name: \(Z^{\mathrm{SN}}_n\)
symbols.def: Empirically self-normalized statistic with a zero-local-variance fallback.

symbols.name: \(\widehat s_{n,\mathrm{loc}}\)
symbols.def: Empirical local-score standard error used only in the critical self-normalized map.

symbols.name: \(x_0\)
symbols.def: Degenerate baseline covariate in the critical submodel.

symbols.name: \(\rho\)
symbols.def: Natural initiation hazard in the critical submodel.

symbols.name: \(r_0\)
symbols.def: Untreated terminal-event hazard in the critical submodel.

symbols.name: \(r_1\)
symbols.def: Post-initiation terminal-event hazard in the critical submodel.

symbols.name: \(\mathcal P_s(L)\)
symbols.def: Observed-law exact-time model with a compatible causal extension.

symbols.name: \(C^{\mathrm{BA}}_{n,\alpha}[b_n]\)
symbols.def: Candidate bias-aware interval for the specified bandwidth sequence.

symbols.name: \(\mathcal B^G_\alpha\)
symbols.def: Diverging-effective-count bandwidth sequences whose stated bias-aware procedure is honest.

assumptions.condition: Under \(P_{\mathrm{full}}\), \(T=T^U\) almost surely when natural initiation occurs while eligible and uncensored.

For every \(u\), the full-filtration intervention that suppresses initiation before \(u\) and initiates at \(u\) when eligible has terminal-event hazards \(\alpha_{02,P}\) before \(u\) and \(\alpha_{12,P}\) after \(u\).

novel.justification: This observed-hazard causal-validity restriction is credible when recorded clinical history contains the prognostic information used for initiation. Correspondence between the full-filtration intervention and the observed-hazard construction is assumed rather than recovered from the observed law.

assumptions.condition: \(\lambda_{C,P_{\mathrm{full}}}(t\mid\mathcal F^{\mathrm{full}}_{t-})=\lambda_{C,P}(t\mid\mathcal H_{t-})\) \(dt\times P_{\mathrm{full}}\)-almost everywhere, where \(\lambda_{C,P}(t\mid\mathcal H_{t-})\) is \(\mathcal H_{t-}\)-predictable.
For almost every realized \(X=x\) under \(P\), \(\inf_{|u-u_\star|\le\delta}\alpha_{01,P}(u\mid x)>0\).

novel.justification: This fixed-window bracket condition is the exact finite-information requirement for the displayed score, retaining predictable risk indicators through its martingale brackets. It permits covariate strata with small, but positive, initiation and censoring survival probabilities and is checkable in bounded finite-dimensional hazard models.

assumptions.condition: \([u\mapsto\Psi_u(P)]\cap\mathcal H_s(L)\ne\varnothing\) as an \(f_{U,P}\)-almost-everywhere equivalence class on \([u_\star-\delta,u_\star+\delta]\).

novel.justification: The observed law must admit a full-data extension satisfying the explicitly displayed causal and censoring restrictions. This permits nonidentified full data while requiring the causal target to be invariant across every compatible extension.

definitions.name: \(\mathcal H_s(L)\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal H_s(L)=\{h:[u_\star-\delta,u_\star+\delta]\to[0,1]: |h(x)-h(y)|\le L|x-y|^s\text{ for }0<s\le1;\ h\in C^1,\ |h'(x)-h'(y)|\le L|x-y|^{s-1}\text{ for }1<s\le2\}\).

definitions.name: \(\mathfrak E(P)\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathfrak E(P)=\{P_{\mathrm{full}}:P_{\mathrm{full}}|_{\mathcal O}=P,\ P_{\mathrm{full}}\text{ satisfies ass:consistency, ass:observed-hazard-validity, and ass:car}\}\).

definitions.name: \(q_b,\overline Q_b,a_b,S^b_{0,P},R_b(P)\)
definitions.construction: \(q_b(u)=b^{-1}K((u-u_\star)/b)\mathbf 1\{|u-u_\star|\le b\},\ \overline Q_b(t)=1-\int_0^tq_b(r)dr,\ a_b(t)=q_b(t)/\overline Q_b(t)\text{ on }\{\overline Q_b(t)>0\},\ S^b_{0,P}(t\mid x)=\overline Q_b(t)\exp\{-\int_0^t\alpha_{02,P}(r\mid x)dr\},\ R_b(P)=E_P[\int_0^{t_0}S^b_{0,P}(t\mid X)\alpha_{02,P}(t\mid X)dt+\int_0^{t_0}q_b(u)\exp\{-\int_0^u\alpha_{02,P}(r\mid X)dr\}V_{1,P}(u,u,X)du]\). Once \(\overline Q_b\) reaches zero, the untreated state is unavailable and its mass is absorbed into the post-initiation state; \(a_b\) is never evaluated there.

definitions.name: \(S^o_{0,P},G_{0,P},G_{1,P},V_{0,P,b},V_{1,P},W_{0,P,b},W_{1,P,b},\phi^q_{P,b}\)
definitions.construction: The pre-initiation and post-initiation censoring hazards \(\alpha_{C0,P}\) and \(\alpha_{C1,P}\) are the restrictions of the observed-history intensity \(\lambda_{C,P}(t\mid\mathcal H_{t-})\) to the corresponding state histories. \(S^o_{0,P}(t\mid x)=\exp\{-\int_0^t[\alpha_{01,P}(r\mid x)+\alpha_{02,P}(r\mid x)]dr\},\ G_{0,P}(t\mid x)=\exp\{-\int_0^t\alpha_{C0,P}(r\mid x)dr\},\ G_{1,P}(t,u\mid x)=\exp\{-\int_u^t\alpha_{C1,P}(r\mid u,x)dr\},\ V_{1,P}(t,u,x)=1-\exp\{-\int_t^{t_0}\alpha_{12,P}(r\mid u,x)dr\},\ V_{0,P,b}(t,x)=\int_t^{t_0}\frac{S^b_{0,P}(r\mid x)}{S^b_{0,P}(t\mid x)}\alpha_{02,P}(r\mid x)dr+\int_t^{t_0}\frac{S^b_{0,P}(u\mid x)}{S^b_{0,P}(t\mid x)}a_b(u)V_{1,P}(u,u,x)du\) on \(A_0\), and \(V_{0,P,b}=0\) off \(A_0\). \(W_{0,P,b}(t,x)=S^b_{0,P}(t\mid x)/[S^o_{0,P}(t\mid x)G_{0,P}(t\mid x)]\) on \(A_0\), and \(W_{0,P,b}=0\) off \(A_0\). On \(A_1\), \(W_{1,P,b}(t,u,x)=S^b_{0,P}(u\mid x)a_b(u)/[S^o_{0,P}(u\mid x)\alpha_{01,P}(u\mid x)G_{0,P}(u\mid x)G_{1,P}(t,u\mid x)]\), and \(W_{1,P,b}=0\) off \(A_1\), including exhaustion endpoints. Set every inactive or endpoint martingale integrand to zero and define \(\phi^q_{P,b}(O)=V_{0,P,b}(0,X)-R_b(P)+\int_0^{t_0}W_{0,P,b}(t,X)[1-V_{0,P,b}(t,X)]dM_{02,P}(t)+\int_0^{t_0}W_{1,P,b}(t,U,X)[1-V_{1,P}(t,U,X)]dM_{12,P}(t)\).

definitions.name: \(\widetilde R_{b_n},\widehat R_{b_n},\widehat v_{b_n},\widehat s_n\)
definitions.construction: \(\widetilde R_{b_n}=R_{b_n}(P)+n^{-1}\sum_{i=1}^n\phi^q_{P,b_n}(O_i)\). For \(\widehat R_{b_n}\), split the sample into a fixed number of folds, fit every hazard in def:eif-components on each complement, evaluate the resulting held-out score \(\widehat\phi^q_{i,b_n}\), set \(\widehat R_{b_n}=n^{-1}\sum_{i=1}^n[\widehat R^{(-i)}_{b_n}+\widehat\phi^q_{i,b_n}]\), \(\widehat v_{b_n}=n^{-1}\sum_{i=1}^n(\widehat\phi^q_{i,b_n}-n^{-1}\sum_{j=1}^n\widehat\phi^q_{j,b_n})^2\), and \(\widehat s_n=\sqrt{\widehat v_{b_n}/n}\).

definitions.name: \(P^{(n)}=P_0\)
definitions.construction: \(P^{(n)}=P_0\) for every \(n\), where \(X=x_0\) almost surely, \(\alpha_{01,P_0}(t)=\rho\), \(\alpha_{02,P_0}(t)=r_0\), \(\alpha_{12,P_0}(t,u)=r_1\), and \(\alpha_{C0,P_0}=\alpha_{C1,P_0}=0\) on \([0,\tau]\), with \(0<\rho,r_0,r_1<H\), \(b_n=\lambda/n\), and \(u_\star+\delta<t_0\). The no-censoring restriction is an uncited constructed submodel restriction.

definitions.name: \(\zeta_n,\widehat\zeta_n,\Xi_n,\widehat\Xi_n,p_1,c,H_{P_0},Z^o_n,Z^{\mathrm{SN}}_n,\nu_{P_0,\lambda},\Gamma^o_\lambda,\Gamma^{\mathrm{SN}}_\lambda\)
definitions.construction:
\(\zeta_n(O)=b_n\phi^q_{P_0,b_n}(O),\ \widehat\zeta_n(O_i)=b_n\widehat\phi^q_{i,b_n},\ \Xi_n=\sum_{i=1}^n\mathbf1\{|U_i-u_\star|\le b_n\}\delta_{((U_i-u_\star)/b_n,\zeta_n(O_i))},\ \widehat\Xi_n=\sum_{i=1}^n\mathbf1\{|U_i-u_\star|\le b_n\}\delta_{((U_i-u_\star)/b_n,\widehat\zeta_n(O_i))},\ v_{P,b}=\operatorname{Var}_P\{\phi^q_{P,b}(O)\},\ s^o_n=\sqrt{v_{P_0,b_n}/n},\ Z^o_n=(\widetilde R_{b_n}-R_{b_n}(P_0))/s^o_n\). In def:critical-submodel, set \(p_1=1-e^{-r_1(t_0-u_\star)}\), \(c(v)=K(v)e^{ho u_\star}/ho\), and \(H_{P_0}(dz\mid v)=p_1\delta_{c(v)(1-p_1)}(dz)+(1-p_1)\delta_{-c(v)p_1}(dz)\). Let \(
u_{P_0,\lambda}(dv,dz)=\lambdaho e^{-(ho+r_0)u_\star}\mathbf1\{|v|\le1\}dvH_{P_0}(dz\mid v)\), \(B_{P_0}=\int z^2d
u_{P_0,\lambda}/\lambda\), and \(\Gamma^o_\lambda(\xi)=\{\lambda B_{P_0}\}^{-1/2}\int z[\xi-
u_{P_0,\lambda}](dv,dz)\). Set \( ar z_\xi=\xi([-1,1]	imes\mathbb R)^{-1}\int z\,d\xi\) when the count is positive. The empirical local variance is \(\widehat s_{n,\mathrm{loc}}^2=(n b_n)^{-2}\int(z- ar z_{\widehat\Xi_n})^2d\widehat\Xi_n\); set \(Z^{\mathrm{SN}}_n=(\widehat R_{b_n}-R_{b_n}(P_0))/\widehat s_{n,\mathrm{loc}}\) if \(\widehat s_{n,\mathrm{loc}}>0\), and \(Z^{\mathrm{SN}}_n=(\widehat R_{b_n}-R_{b_n}(P_0))/s^o_n\) otherwise. Define \(\Gamma^{\mathrm{SN}}_\lambda(\xi)=\int z[\xi-
u_{P_0,\lambda}](dv,dz)/\sqrt{\int(z- ar z_\xi)^2d\xi}\) when its denominator is positive, and \(\Gamma^{\mathrm{SN}}_\lambda(\xi)=\Gamma^o_\lambda(\xi)\) otherwise. The candidate Laplace-functional equation is \(E[\exp\{-\int h\,d\mathsf N_{P_0,\lambda}\}]=\exp\{-\int(1-e^{-h})d
u_{P_0,\lambda}\}\) for bounded nonnegative \(h\).

definitions.name: \(\widetilde R^{\,G}_{b_n},s^o_{G,n},Z^o_{G,n}\)
definitions.construction: For a triangular family \(P_G^{(n)}\in\mathcal P_s(L)\) with \(b_n\downarrow0\) and \(nb_n\to\infty\), let \(O_{n1},\ldots,O_{nn}\stackrel{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}P_G^{(n)}\), \(\widetilde R^{\,G}_{b_n}=R_{b_n}(P_G^{(n)})+n^{-1}\sum_{i=1}^n\phi^q_{P_G^{(n)},b_n}(O_{ni})\), \(s^o_{G,n}=\sqrt{v_{P_G^{(n)},b_n}/n}\), and \(Z^o_{G,n}=[\widetilde R^{\,G}_{b_n}-R_{b_n}(P_G^{(n)})]/s^o_{G,n}\).

definitions.name: \(\mathcal P_s(L)\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal P_s(L)=\{P:\text{ass:baseline-eligibility, ass:bounded-hazards, ass:conditional-initiation-positivity, ass:interior, ass:holder-version, and ass:compatible-extension hold for }P\}\).

definitions.name: \(B_s(b),C^{\mathrm{BA}}_{n,\alpha}[b_n],\mathcal C_{n,\alpha},D_n,D^\star_{n,\alpha},\mathcal B^G_\alpha\)
definitions.construction: \(B_s(b)=L b^s\int|v|^sK(v)dv\) for \(0<s\le1\), and \(B_s(b)=(L/s)b^s\int|v|^sK(v)dv\) for \(1<s\le2\). Let \(\operatorname{cv}_\alpha(r)\) be the \(1-\alpha\) quantile of \(|Z+r|\) for \(Z\sim N(0,1)\), and set \(C^{\mathrm{BA}}_{n,\alpha}[b_n]=[\widehat R_{b_n}\pm\operatorname{cv}_\alpha(B_s(b_n)/\widehat s_n)\widehat s_n]\). Also \(\mathcal C_{n,\alpha}=\{C_n:\liminf_{n\to\infty}\inf_{P\in\mathcal P_s(L)}P^n[\theta(P)\in C_n]\ge1-\alpha\}\), \(D_n(C_n)=\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_s(L)}E_P[\operatorname{diam}(C_n)]\), \(D^\star_{n,\alpha}=\inf_{C_n\in\mathcal C_{n,\alpha}}D_n(C_n)\), and \(\mathcal B^G_\alpha=\{(b_n):b_n\downarrow0,nb_n\to\infty,C^{\mathrm{BA}}_{n,\alpha}[b_n]\in\mathcal C_{n,\alpha}\}\).

statements.statement: Under ass:consistency, ass:observed-hazard-validity, ass:car, ass:baseline-eligibility, ass:bounded-hazards, ass:conditional-initiation-positivity, ass:holder-version, and ass:compatible-extension, every \(P_{\mathrm{full}}\in\mathfrak E(P)\) satisfies \(P_{\mathrm{full}}(T^u\le t_0)=\Psi_u(P)\) for \(f_{U,P}\)-almost every \(u\). In the baseline-eligible hazard model, \(f_{U,P}(u)=E_P[S^o_{0,P}(u\mid X)\alpha_{01,P}(u\mid X)]\), and for every \(u\in[u_\star-\delta,u_\star+\delta]\), \(f_{U,P}(u)\ge e^{-2H\tau}E_P[\inf_{|r-u_\star|\le\delta}\alpha_{01,P}(r\mid X)]>0\). Thus the common equivalence class has a unique \(g_P\in\mathcal H_s(L)\), and \(\theta(P)=g_P(u_\star)\) is invariant across compatible extensions.
statements.justification: A pointwise causal target needs an observed-law version selection before local inference can be meaningful.
statements.gap: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased, pp. 5--7, regularize a nonregular initiation-time target but do not state this compatible-extension Hölder selection lemma.
statements.consumer: It makes the honesty event in oeq:honest-diameter causal and extension-invariant.

statements.statement: Under ass:iid, ass:car, ass:bounded-hazards, ass:conditional-initiation-positivity, ass:score-integrability, ass:interior, and ass:kernel, the rule in def:exogenous-intervention is a normalized stochastic initiation rule with density \(q_b\), no-initiation mass \(\overline Q_b\) before exhaustion, the flux stated there, and the stated absorbing convention after exhaustion. Direct pathwise differentiation of its product integral gives \(\phi^q_{P,b}\) in def:eif-components as its observed-data efficient influence function; the proof establishes its \(G_{0,P}G_{1,P}\) post-initiation weight, time-dependent \(V_{1,P}\), centering, and the stated uncensored reduction.
statements.justification: The critical experiment requires a score derived for its own planned-hazard intervention.
statements.gap: Sections 3.1 and 4 derive EIFs solely for their own natural-hazard-dependent normalized-window intervention; the externally fixed-\(q_b\) EIF here is independently pathwise differentiated and is not obtained by substitution.
statements.consumer: It fixes the oracle marks that oeq:critical-phase must analyze.

statements.statement: Under lem:extension-invariance, ass:holder-version, ass:interior, and ass:kernel, \(R_b(P)=\int q_b(u)g_P(u)du\) and \(|R_b(P)-\theta(P)|\le B_s(b)\), with \(B_s(b)\) defined in def:bias-aware-interval. For \(1<s\le2\), symmetry of \(K\) cancels the first-order term.
statements.justification: This gives the exact Hölder bias envelope needed to state the confidence problem without presuming Gaussian approximation.
statements.gap: ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI give this local-regression bias geometry; its product-integral identity for a CAR initiation intervention is not supplied there.
statements.consumer: It supplies the bias term in the formal interval of oeq:honest-diameter.

statements.statement: Let \(A_0=\{t:\overline Q_b(t)>0\}\) and \(A_1=\{(t,u):q_b(u)>0,\overline Q_b(u)>0,u\le t\le t_0\}\). If \(\alpha_{01,P}=a_b\) on \(A_0\) and \(\alpha_{C0,P}=\alpha_{C1,P}=0\), then \(W_{0,P,b}=1\) on \(A_0\) and \(W_{1,P,b}=1\) on \(A_1\). Both weights, and hence their martingale integrands, are zero off their respective active supports under def:eif-components; equivalently, the globally defined weights reduce to their active-support indicators, and \(\phi^q_{P,b}\) is the uncensored g-computation martingale gradient for \(R_b(P)\).
statements.justification: This checks the direct score against the standard no-censoring and no-reweighting special case.
statements.gap: This is a reduction check rather than a theorem-level comparison to a published estimator.
statements.consumer: It is a diagnostic benchmark for the pathwise differentiation in thm:exogenous-eif.

statements.statement: For def:critical-submodel, derive the critical experiment from thm:exogenous-eif: prove the conditional mark kernel \(H_{P_0}\), the Laplace-functional convergence \(\Xi_n\Rightarrow\mathsf N_{P_0,\lambda}\), and the continuous-mapping limits \(Z^o_n\Rightarrow\Gamma^o_\lambda(\mathsf N_{P_0,\lambda})\) and, with the displayed zero-local-variance fallback, \(Z^{\mathrm{SN}}_n\Rightarrow\Gamma^{\mathrm{SN}}_\lambda(\mathsf N_{P_0,\lambda})\). Establish the no-local-initiation atom and non-Gaussianity. Separately, for def:diverging-count-statistic, derive a Gaussian limit for \(Z^o_{G,n}\) conditional on verifying the triangular Lindeberg condition \(v_{P_G^{(n)},b_n}^{-1}E_{P_G^{(n)}}[\{\phi^q_{P_G^{(n)},b_n}(O)\}^2\mathbf1\{|\phi^q_{P_G^{(n)},b_n}(O)|>\epsilon\sqrt{n v_{P_G^{(n)},b_n}}\}]\to0\) for every \(\epsilon>0\), deterministic variance stabilization and nondegeneracy \(0<\liminf_n b_nv_{P_G^{(n)},b_n}\le\limsup_n b_nv_{P_G^{(n)},b_n}<\infty\), and separately derived componentwise cross-fitted hazard, censoring, delayed-entry survival, score-remainder, and uniform-integrability conditions, including \(\widehat R_{b_n}-\widetilde R^{\,G}_{b_n}=o_P(s^o_{G,n})\).
statements.justification: The focal novelty is a rare-initiation multistate limit experiment, with a separate diverging-count Gaussian problem and a critical self-normalized law.
statements.gap: SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous, Theorems 2 and 4, treat concentration for static exposures; AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment, Sections 1--2, treats ordinary continuous-treatment Gaussian bands. Neither constructs this CAR illness-death marked-Poisson experiment.
statements.consumer: It determines whether bounded local initiation counts require marked-Poisson calibration and what must be verified before Wald calibration is available at diverging counts.

statements.statement: Determine whether \(\mathcal B^G_\alpha\) is nonempty, characterize its boundary, and determine the order of \(\inf_{(b_n)\in\mathcal B^G_\alpha}D_n(C^{\mathrm{BA}}_{n,\alpha}[b_n])\) relative to \(D^\star_{n,\alpha}\). When \(nb_n=O(1)\), determine a confidence construction and diameter benchmark calibrated to the critical law derived in oeq:critical-phase.
statements.justification: Exact-time confidence procedures must combine Hölder bias with the effective-count phase transition.
statements.gap: ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI, Sections 1--4, derive bias-aware local-regression interval geometry, and MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased use fixed-window Wald inference; neither identifies the bounded-count CAR illness-death frontier.
statements.consumer: It decides whether the 1,896-couple IUI timing analysis can report a local exact-time interval or must retain a regularized-window target.

sampling_model.units: \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\) are baseline eligible and have one possible initiation and right censoring.
sampling_model.treatment: The analytical rule replaces the at-risk initiation hazard by \(a_b=q_b/\overline Q_b\) only before \(\overline Q_b\) is exhausted.
sampling_model.overlap: Conditional natural initiation positivity holds for every covariate stratum treated by \(q_b\), and the displayed score is square-integrable.
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: Cross-fitting fits observed transition, censoring, and delayed-entry survival hazards; oracle equivalence in both the critical and diverging-count triangular families is part of oeq:critical-phase.

tldr: This proposal studies the effective-count phase transition of a planned, exogenous initiation-time intervention in a CAR-censored illness-death model. A Hölder version restriction makes exact-time terminal-event risk an extension-invariant causal target. The settled substrate is a direct product-integral and EIF derivation for a hazard defined only while the planned no-initiation mass is positive. The open phase question separates a critical marked-Poisson experiment from a diverging-count Gaussian experiment and then asks when cross-fitted empirical normalization preserves each limit.

project_justification.gap: Fixed-window survival intervention theory and continuous-treatment effective-count theory do not yield a rare-initiation experiment for a learned CAR multistate score.
project_justification.niche: The exogenous at-risk hazard creates a normalized, localized intervention whose critical regime has finitely many initiation marks.
project_justification.fill: The proposal derives the correct score substrate, specifies a nonempty triangular law and self-normalized statistic, and poses the phase and honest-diameter conclusions as constructions rather than assumptions.

related_work: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased, Sections 3.1 and 4 derive EIFs solely for their own natural-hazard-dependent normalized-window intervention; the externally fixed-\(q_b\) EIF here is independently pathwise differentiated and is not obtained by substitution. SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous, Theorems 2 and 4, tracks concentration for static continuous exposures without censoring-adjusted multistate marks. AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment, Sections 1--2, gives weighted local-linear uniform inference for ordinary continuous-treatment response processes, not a bounded-effective-count illness-death point process. ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI, Sections 1--4, supplies worst-case-Hölder-bias interval geometry for local regression, without learned CAR multistate scores. ColangeloLee2023ContinuousDML supplies a kernel-localized cross-fitted continuous-treatment template, while WestlingLuedtkeGilbertCarone2024Survival supplies survival-EIF inference without shrinking initiation windows. KennedyMaMcHughSmall2017ContinuousDR introduces kernel-smoothed doubly robust continuous-treatment curves, but not the present multistate intervention or critical regime.
honest_scope: The causal interpretation is conditional on every compatible full-data extension satisfying the displayed restrictions. The proposition-level claims concern extension-invariance, the legal planned intervention, its direct score, its bias identity, and an uncensored reduction. Gaussian limits in the separately defined diverging-count family, marked-Poisson limits in the critical family, plug-in equivalence, confidence honesty, and any optimal-diameter conclusion remain open. The IUI analysis is an applied consumer only and makes no exact-day finding.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Sections 3.1 and 4 derive EIFs solely for their natural-hazard-dependent normalized-window intervention.
Static continuous-exposure incremental effects have concentration-dependent effective-sample-size theory.
Weighted local-linear continuous-treatment estimators admit uniform inference and bootstrap bands.
Worst-case-Hölder-bias local-regression intervals have honesty and length comparisons.
Kernel-localized cross-fitted DML gives continuous-treatment inference under nuisance-rate conditions.
Cross-fitted survival-EIF methods yield treatment-specific survival inference without shrinking initiation interventions.
Kernel-smoothed doubly robust estimators identify continuous-treatment response curves.

novelty_justification.repo_axis: The closest in-catalogue objects study bounded-count confidence diameters for principal-stratum ratios and continuous-index minimax localization. Neither defines an exogenous illness-death planned-hazard score or its marked initiation point process.
novelty_justification.published_axis: The cited survival, continuous-treatment, concentration, and honest-CI papers separately supply adjacent ingredients. The unsolved intersection is a directly derived CAR multistate score and its finite-effective-count self-normalized marked-Poisson experiment.

literature_checklist.author: Martinussen, Knudsen, and Rytgaard
literature_checklist.one_line: Defines a distinct natural-initiation-window intervention and its covariate-adjusted fixed-window EIF.

literature_checklist.author: Schindl, Shen, and Kennedy
literature_checklist.one_line: Studies concentration and effective sample size for static continuous exposures.

literature_checklist.author: Ai, Huang, and Zhang
literature_checklist.one_line: Gives uniform continuous-treatment response-process inference without multistate critical limits.

literature_checklist.author: Colangelo and Lee
literature_checklist.venue: Journal of Business and Economic Statistics
literature_checklist.one_line: Provides cross-fitted localized continuous-treatment DML theory.

literature_checklist.author: Westling et al.
literature_checklist.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
literature_checklist.one_line: Provides survival-EIF inference without shrinking initiation-time localization.

literature_checklist.author: Kennedy, Ma, McHugh, and Small
literature_checklist.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B
literature_checklist.one_line: Introduces kernel-smoothed doubly robust continuous-treatment response estimation.
